title: Communauté de communes Loches Sud Touraine
summary: The Communauté de communes Loches Sud Touraine is a communauté de communes, an intercommunal structure, in the Indre-et-Loire department, in the Centre-Val de Loire region, central France. It was created on 1 January 2017 by the merger of the former communautés de communes of Loches Développement, Montrésor, Grand Ligueillois and Touraine du Sud. Its area is 1,809.5 km2, and its population was 51,376 in 2018. Its seat is in Loches.
text:
The Communauté de communes Loches Sud Touraine is a communauté de communes, an intercommunal structure, in the Indre-et-Loire department, in the Centre-Val de Loire region, central France. It was created on 1 January 2017 by the merger of the former communautés de communes of Loches Développement, Montrésor, Grand Ligueillois and Touraine du Sud. Its area is 1,809.5 km2, and its population was 51,376 in 2018. Its seat is in Loches.

Composition
The communauté de communes consists of the following 67 communes:
